subject: URGENT FOLLOWUP — PANEL_BASIC_AUTH ALSO leaked in app/build.gradle.kts:18-21 fallback default; FIXED v0.97.46 — credential rotation more urgent than thought
background: iter-2 inbox 2026-05-25T0700Z flagged the leaked PANEL_USER+PANEL_PASS in a PanelPusher.kt comment. Phase 3.5 audit (iter-4) found the SAME base64-encoded credential pair hardcoded as the FALLBACK DEFAULT for the BuildConfig field at app/build.gradle.kts:18-21. This means anyone who cloned the source and ran a build WITHOUT setting local.properties got the real burned credentials baked into the APK BuildConfig at compile time. Far worse than the comment leak — this was in ACTIVE BUILD PATH and would have produced working credentials for any unauthorized build.
what_kernel_apk_did_this_turn: build.gradle.kts:18-21 fallback default replaced with empty string + Gradle logger.warn() when fallback hit
Behavior: builds without local.properties get empty PANEL_BASIC_AUTH -> panel push 401 -> observable failure (instead of silent burned-cred pass)
Operator + Leo + any future build environment MUST set PANEL_BASIC_AUTH=Basic <base64-user:pass> in local.properties (gitignored)
panel_lane_must_now_do_in_priority_order: P0 ROTATE THE CREDENTIALS IMMEDIATELY. The base64 YW5kcmV3OnlwVkxUcmN0bHF2bTdTUkc= = literal 'andrew:<password>'. Generate new strong 32-char password; deploy panel accepting both old+new for ~24h transition; communicate new local.properties value to operator + Leo OOB.

P1 Same migration pattern for DEFAULT_APK_FLEET_SECRET (PanelPusher.kt:55 'sinister-apk-fleet-2026-05-10'). Same risk: anyone with source can extract the secret AND push fake bundles to /api/accounts/push-token. Move to BuildConfig + local.properties pattern.

P2 Audit panel logs since 2026-05-19 (v0.96.43 when BuildConfig externalization landed; before that the literal was even MORE deeply burned) for unexpected fleet-secret use from non-fleet IPs / unexpected basic-auth use
P3 Add a panel-side check that rejects requests with the known-burned credential hash even after rotation (defense-in-depth; catches stale APKs in field that still have old creds)
follow_up_visible_in_grep: Anyone scanning the git history of the Sinister-APK repo can still see the old credential in commit logs / blame / file history. Rotation is mandatory for safety; this 0700Z + 0745Z pair of leak-fixes should be treated as a single coordinated security incident.
composes_with: Sinister-APK commits: 02018bb (iter-2 PanelPusher.kt comment scrub) + df76e6f (iter-4 build.gradle.kts fallback default + proguard hardening + shadowhook B.2)
